# Worthless

> Worthless is an open-source tool that splits your API keys so a leaked key can't do anything. It's not a vault. Even if the key leaks, it won't work.

## What does Worthless do?

Worthless splits your API key into two shares using XOR secret sharing. One share stays on your machine (.env), the other goes to a local proxy. Neither share works alone. When your app makes an API call, the proxy combines both shares for a split second, calls the provider, then destroys the key. A leaked .env contains only a useless share.

## Why use Worthless?

API keys leak constantly. AI agents run `git add -A` and push your .env to GitHub. CI logs print keys. Compromised packages read process.env. Vaults protect keys at rest, but the key is still one piece in memory. Worthless is different: the key in your .env is only half a key. Even if it leaks, it's mathematically useless.

## Supported providers

- OpenAI (sk-proj-* keys)
- Anthropic (sk-ant-* keys)
- Gemini (planned)

## Quick start (3 commands)

```bash
pip install worthless
worthless lock
worthless wrap python app.py
```

1. `pip install worthless` — install the CLI
2. `worthless lock` — scans .env, splits each API key into two shares
3. `worthless wrap python app.py` — runs your app with the proxy injecting the reconstructed key

Your code needs zero changes. Environment variables work exactly as before.

## How to uninstall

```bash
worthless unlock
```

One command. Original .env restored. Proxy stopped. Shares deleted. Zero artifacts left behind. No lock-in.

## Key features

- Split-key cryptography (XOR, information-theoretically secure)
- Hard spending cap per key (proxy refuses to reconstruct if budget exceeded)
- Pre-commit scanning (blocks commits with raw API keys)
- Zero code changes (proxy injects keys transparently)
- Fully reversible (`worthless unlock` restores everything)
- Self-hosted, open source (AGPL-3.0), no cloud, no account, zero telemetry

## Not a vault

Worthless is complementary to vaults, not a replacement. Use a vault AND Worthless. The vault prevents leaks. Worthless makes leaks harmless. They solve different problems.

## Pages

- [Home](https://worthless.cloud/): Product overview
- [Features](https://worthless.cloud/features.html): Full feature breakdown
- [How It Works](https://worthless.cloud/how-it-works.html): Architecture and security model
- [Blog](https://worthless.cloud/blog/): Articles on API key security
- [Red Blog](https://worthless.cloud/red/): Attack scenarios from the attacker's POV
- [Memes](https://worthless.cloud/memes.html): Security memes
